title: Raborn Decision Sciences
text:
Raborn Decision Sciences helps individuals and organizations translate complex measurement, operational, and planning questions into defensible analyses, reproducible workflows, and usable decision-support tools.
Explore services View tools Contact me

title: Raborn Decision Sciences
text:
What I do



Assessment & Psychometrics
Item analysis, form analysis, DIF, equating, standard setting support, technical reporting, and validity evidence.




R/Shiny Decision Tools
Custom interactive applications, Quarto reporting workflows, R package development and support, dashboards, and analytic automation.




Operations & Planning Models
Break-even tools, capacity planning, pricing scenarios, and interactive models for service-based organizations.




Decision point: The goal is not just analysis. The goal is a tool, workflow, or report that helps someone make a clearer decision.

title: Raborn Decision Sciences
text:
Expert-led quantitative consulting
Raborn Decision Sciences is led by Anthony Raborn, a psychometrician/statistician focused on applied measurement, reproducible analytics, and custom R/Shiny tools.
About Anthony

title: About
text:
Anthony Raborn is a psychometrician/statistician specializing in applied measurement, reproducible analytics, and custom R/Shiny decision tools.
My work combines psychometric methods, statistical programming, and practical software development to help organizations make clearer decisions from complex assessment and operational data. I strive to provide tools that make work easier to repeat, explain, and use for all levels of technical expertise.

title: About
text:

PhD in Research and Evaluation Methodology, Minor in Statistics

title: Tools and Projects
text: Raborn Decision Sciences has been involved in the creation of multiple free and open source products for performing high-quality, replicable analyses.

title: Tools and Projects
text:
ShortForm
ShortForm is an R package for automated short form development, combining psychometric methods, machine learning, and reproducible statistical workflows.
It uses proven, peer-reviewed metaheuristic algorithms to shorten assessents, scales, and other measurement tools while preserving psychometric and statistical qualities. The package documentation is available for everyone to read. The source code is also publically available on GitHub.
Raborn Decision Sciences has also developed a Shiny app to demonstrate the ShortForm package with simulated data.

title: Tools and Projects
text:
Direct Care Break-Even App
An interactive Shiny planning tool for modeling revenue, capacity, pricing, and break-even scenarios in direct care or service-based medical practices.

title: Tools and Projects
text:
Psychometric Quarto Reporting App
An interactive Shiny app that produces a branded technical report in .docx, .html, and .pdf formats.

title: Tools and Projects
text:
Programmatic Tools
Some of the tools used to create these products were:

R, an open source statistical programming language.
Shiny, an open source software for creating web applications.
Quarto, an open source scientific and technical publishing system.
LaTeX, an open source document preparation system.
Git, an open source distributed version control system.
Fedora Linux, an open source operating system using the Linux kernel.
Positron and RStudio, integrated development environments for R and other languages.

This website itself is built on a combination of Quarto, Git, and Positron. Its code lives in a GitHub repository and is published via Quarto within a continuous integration/continuous deployment process.

title: Shiny Short Form
text: ShinyShortForm is a small app demonstrating the ShortForm package. It allows users to modify some of the parameters of the metaheuristic algorithms included in the package and see first-hand how they affect the runtime and output using simulated sample data.
